channel.title: David J Berry on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in David J Berry on Berrytree.org

item.title: Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Preface | Table of Contents | Chapter 2: Robert Berry →

Chapter 1#

Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps#

I have been researching the Robert Berry family from Orange County since 1997. When I first started researching, I found that the Census forms from 1790 to 1840, only listed the head of household’s given and surnames. Sometimes they would be listed alphabetically, which was not totally useless but not very helpful either. When the families were listed in the order of farms and plantation physical location, the information became more valuable. I would search the census forms from the very early years and become familiar with as many of the surnames located near one of my known ancestors as possible. Soon I learned that a cluster of surnames in one county would often vary greatly from the surnames listed in the very next county. Since the population was small, clusters of surnames in a close proximity were often related to each other. As our ancestors relocated from one state to another, the pattern was repeated over and over. When I located a family surname who seemed to be a relative from a previous community, I started looking at the surnames of his neighbors. I always paid particular attention to these very close neighbors. This is a very important tool; since there were really not very many people in these early settlements, many of them migrated there together. Many of them were friends and a lot of them were relatives and children who had known each other their entire lives. They had grown up together and when the time came to relocate, they chose to go together.

The Story of Cora Berry McVicker#

item.title: Chapter 2: Robert Berry 1729–1814
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys, Kemps | Table of Contents | Chapter 3: Mary Berry →

Chapter 2#

Robert Berry 1729–1814#

Robert Berry OC came to a part of Granville County, North Carolina, which was soon to become Orange County, NC. We know that he built a small cabin in the northeast corner of his staked out land. This cabin was documented in Robert Berry’s OC Entry for his Land Grant. Thomas Bradford owned the land just north of Robert Berry’s OC claim.

item.title: Robert Berry (OC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (OC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 Robert Berry

Born
c. 1729, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Died
1814, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Elizabeth Cate — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
Robert Berry and Mary Williamson, Princess Anne County, VA
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry#

Robert Berry was the son of Robert Berry and Mary Williamson of Princess Anne County, Virginia, and he came to Orange County, North Carolina, in 1751.

item.title: Elizabeth Cate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Elizabeth Cate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 2 (by marriage) Elizabeth Cate Berry

Born
c. 1735, Virginia
Died
c. 1817, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Robert Berry — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
John and Margery Lawrence Cate, Prince George County, Virginia
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Elizabeth Cate Berry#

Elizabeth Cate was the daughter of John and Margery Cate. Her oldest brother was Joshua Cate and her youngest brother was Thomas Cate, who was a chain carrier when Robert Berry’s Granville Grant was surveyed in 1753. Robert Berry and Elizabeth Cate were married about 1759. At least three of their children were already married before marriage records were kept in Hillsborough. The ages of their children would indicate that their marriage took place around 1759.

item.title: Robert Berry (PAC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (PAC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 1 Robert Berry (PAC)

Born
Unknown — probably born in England
Died
c. 1730, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Married
Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Parents
Unknown — most likely born in England
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry (PAC)#

Robert Berry (PAC) of South Norfolk County, Virginia, married Mary Williamson and established a home in Princess Anne County, Virginia, which is now known as Virginia Beach, Virginia. Robert and Mary had two sons. The oldest was Richard Berry and the baby was Robert Berry “Orange County, NC” (OC) who was born about 1729. Robert Berry (PAC) wrote his will in 1730 and died shortly after Robert Berry (Orange County, NC) was born.

item.title: Chapter 10: David J. Berry 1774–1838
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 9: Thomas Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 11: William Berry →

Ancestor Profile: David J. Berry Sr. — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 10#

David J. Berry 1774 to 1838#

David J. Berry was the eighth child and the sixth son born to Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry. The Regulator Movement had already been quieted by the brutal and unfair actions of Governor Tryon three years before David was born. David was in his formative years while the Revolutionary War was being waged. David was about 6 years old when his father, Robert Berry, his oldest brother, Robert Berry Jr., and brother-in-law, George Waggoner, joined the North Carolina Continental Army. George Waggoner, who married David’s older sister Mary Berry, was apparently mortally wounded in the battle at Guilford Court House. It is very hard to imagine how bad conditions were, what the family had to endure, or the effect it may have had on David as he was growing up.

item.title: Roger Williamson PAC
item.description:
Roger Williamson & Ann Foster

Early settlers of Princess Anne County, Virginia, from England. Earliest known Berry ancestors in America.

Roger Williamson from England and Princess Ann County, Virginia#

PARENTS of Roger Williamson and Ann Foster are unknown at this time.

item.description:
1. Home

1729, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Died
1814, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC
Married
Elizabeth Cate
Parents
Robert Berry (PAC) and Mary Williamson
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Ancestors of Robert Berry (OC)#

Maternal Ancestors#

  • Great Grandparents: George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe
  • Great Grandparents: Roger and Ann Foster Williamson
  • Grandparents: Richard and Mary Kempe Williamson
  • Parents: Robert and Mary Williamson Berry

Descendants of Ann Foster and Roger Williamson#

  • Gen 1 Ann Foster (b. 1620, England — d. c. 1692, Parish Church, Lynnhaven Parish, Lower Norfolk County, Virginia)
      • Roger Williamson (b. Unknown — m. June 5, 1646, Virginia — d. bef. 1650, Lower Norfolk Co., VA)
    • ——–Gen 2 Richard Williamson (b. before 1650 — d. 1721, Princess Anne County, Virginia)
        • Mary Kempe (d. Unknown)
      • (Parents: George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe — Kempe’s Landing, Princess Anne County, Virginia)
      • ————–Gen 3 Mary Williamson
          • Robert Berry (b. Unknown — d. 1730; parents unknown)
        • ————————Gen 4 Robert Berry (b. 1729 — d. 1814, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC)
            • Elizabeth Cate (b. 1735 — d. aft. 1816, Fiddleton Plantation, Orange County, NC)
          • (Parents: John and Margery Lawrence Cate)

Children of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry (Gen 5)#

  • ————————————Gen 5 Mary Berry (b. about 1759)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Robert Berry Jr. (b. about 1760)
  • ————————————Gen 5 John Berry (b. about 1762)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Joshua Berry (b. about 1766)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Isaac Berry (b. about 1768)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Elizabeth Berry (b. about 1770)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Thomas Berry (b. about 1772)
  • ————————————Gen 5 David J. Berry (b. about 1774)
  • ————————————Gen 5 William Berry (b. about 1775)
  • ————————————Gen 5 Henry Berry (b. 1776)

Other Children of Richard and Mary Kempe Williamson#

  • ————–Gen 3 John Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Roger Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Bartholomew Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 George Williamson
  • ————–Gen 3 Sarah Williamson
      • Matthew Mathias
  • ————–Gen 3 Ann Williamson
      • Hugh Whitehurst

Other Children of Ann Foster and Roger Williamson#

  • ——–Gen 2 Bartholomew Williamson (b. before 1650)
  • ——–Gen 2 Sarah Williamson (b. before 1650)

Ancestor List#

  • George Kempe — Great Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC) — was born in England
  • Mary Hutchinson Kempe — Great Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Roger Williamson — Great Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Ann Foster Williamson — Great Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC) — born in England in 1620 and died in Princess Anne County, Virginia about 1692
  • Richard Williamson — Grandfather of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Mary Kempe Williamson — Grandmother of Robert Berry (OC)
  • Robert Berry (PAC) — Father of Robert Berry (OC) — died in Princess Anne County, Virginia in 1730
  • Mary Williamson Berry — Mother of Robert Berry (OC) — died in Princess Anne County, Virginia about 1746

item.title: Albert Edgar Berry
item.description:
Albert Edgar Berry, son of Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
  • Gen 4 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — Georgia
  • Gen 5 Jesse Lee Berry × Nancy Ann Henry — Georgia & Alabama
  • Gen 6 Albert Edgar Berry × Ella Claudia Dorton — Georgia

**L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture Albert Edgar Berry **was born on December 28, 1881 in Etowah County Alabama. Albert died om May 23, 1964. He and his first wife are buried at Bethany Baptist Church Cemetery, Henry County, Georgia. Albert Edgar Berry and Ella Claudia Dorton were married on the 23 January 1905. She was born February 2, 1880 and died February 15 1916.

Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 5Basil Manley Berry × Mary Elizabeth Watson — Milton County, Georgia

Generation 5 Basil Manley Berry

Born
June 19, 1856, Milton County, Georgia
Died
October 1, 1914, Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia
Married
Mary Elizabeth Watson (married 1878; born June 24, 1855; died September 13, 1941)
Parents
Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Basil Manley Berry#

Basil Manley Berry born June 19th, 1856 in Milton County, Georgia. In 1878 at the age of 20 he married Mary Elizabeth Watson born June 24, 1855, daughter of William L. Watson and Elizabeth A. ? Watson. Basil Manley Berry died Oct 1, 1914 in Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia and Mary Elizabeth Berry died Sept 13, 1941 in Roswell, Fulton County, Georgia. They are buried at Willeo Cemetery in Cobb County, Georgia.

David J. Berry Jr.#

David J. Berry Jr. is the son of David J. Berry and Mary Blalock. He married Margarett Erwin in Fayette County, Georgia. David J. Berry was born in Orange County, North Carolina.

item.title: Dora Ellen Turley Ga
item.description:
Dora Ellen Turley, daughter of Nathan and Mary Jori Berry Turley#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry Berry
  5. Generation Mary Jori Berry Turley and Nathan Turley

DORA ELLEN TURLEY born April 11, 1874 in Milton County Georgia and died June 6, 1915 after giving birth to twin daughters. She married George Robert Pace before 1892. Dora Ellen Turley Pace is buried in the Holly Springs Cemetery, Cobb County Georgia.

item.title: Elizabeth Leathers Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Elizabeth Leathers Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 3 (stepdaughter) Elizabeth Leathers Berry

Born
c. 1791
Died
Unknown
Married
Unknown
Parents
Mary Blalock and William Riley (biological father); adopted by David J. Berry Sr.
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Elizabeth Leathers Berry, Was The Daughter Of Mary Blalock#

Elizabeth Berry was born before David J. Berry married Mary Blalock. She is not blood related to the Berry family but David J. Berry adopted his step daughter and she was raised with his own children. As far as I know there is no knowledge of who she married or what kind of life she was able to make for herself after David J. Berry and her mother, Mary Blalock, died. Elizabeth was the illegitimate daughter of William Riley, who was brought to court about the matter. A bastardy bond was placed on him. This is the same William Riley against whom Elizabeth Berry (daughter of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry) also filed a bastardy bond in August 1791.

Land transactions and migration from Orange County, NC, through Fayette, Henry, Cobb, and Milton Counties, Georgia.

Henry Berry, Son of David J. Berry#

Henry Berry is the son of David J. Berry and Mary Blalock. He was born in 1810 in Orange County, North Carolina and married Sarah Roberts on March 13th 1883 in Fayette County, Alabama.

December 27, 1823, Henry County, Georgia
Died
February 8, 1891, Ellenwood, Clayton County, Georgia
Married
Serena Crow (married October 10, 1844, Clayton County, GA; born March 20, 1828; died July 27, 1902)
Parents
William Abraham and Sarah Berry Cates
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Isaac A. Cates#

Isaac A. Cates was born Dec. 27, 1823 in Henry County, Georgia. He married Oct. 10, 1844 in Clayton County, Georgia Serena Crow. He died Feb 8, 1891 in Ellenwood, Clayton County, Georgia. Serena was born Mar 20, 1828 in Henry County and died July 27, 1902 in Clayton County, Georgia.

Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Other Generations

James A. (Jim) Berry

Born
December 27, 1922, Perry, Georgia
Died
May 18, 2016, Chula Vista, California
Married
1st: Marjorie 'Tina' Berry — 2nd: Hilda Bridges Berry
Parents
David J. Berry line (specific intermediate generations not listed)
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

James A. (Jim) Berry, Born December 27 1922 Perry Georgia. Died May 18 2016 Chula Vista California.

item.title: Jesse Lee Berry
item.description:
Jesse Lee Berry, son of Robert and Elizabeth Everett Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
  • Gen 4 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — North Carolina & Georgia

JESSE LEE BERRY was born on December 2, 1852 in Roswell, Cobb County Georgia. Jesse Lee married Nancy Ann Henry who was born December 30 1857 in Ellenwood, Clayton Co., Georgia. They were married December 16 1874 in Clayton County Georgia. Nancy was the daughter of Edward Zachary “Zack” Henry and Mary Ann Mitchell. Zack’s Father was Jesse G. Henry and Nancy Hammock. Mary Ann’s father was Thomas Mitchell, son of Hinchey Mitchell and Elizabeth Ruff (daughter of William Ruff and Rebecca). In the picture of Jesse Lee on the right there seems to be a faded date of July 1875 written on the picture. He would have been 22 Yrs 7 Months old.

item.title: Jesse Waldo Maners
item.description:

Born
Guilford County, North Carolina
Died
August 4, 1982, Anderson, Anderson County, South Carolina
Married
Velma Irene Morgan (divorced June 25, 1970)
Parents
Addie Lena Berry and Hampton Fantom Maners, Spalding, GA & Guilford County, NC

John Allmon Berry born July 16th, 1843 in Fayette County, Georgia. He married Sarah Elizabeth (Sally) Starke. They had seven children.

item.title: Mary Jori Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mary Jori Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt Berry — Georgia

Generation 5 Mary Jori Berry

Born
June 11, 1836 (Bible page states 1835)
Died
July 4, 1890, Fayette County, Georgia
Married
Nathan Turley — born August 25, 1838, died December 26, 1923
Parents
Robert Berry and Elizabeth Everitt Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Mary J Berry grew up in the Roswell Georgia area of Milton County Georgia. In 1930 Milton County Georgia was renamed Fulton County Georgia. Mary J. Berry married Nathan Turley born August 25, 1838 and died December 26, 1923. The picture on the right is of Nathan Turley. There are no pictures known to exist of Mary J. Berry. Click on the picture to view a larger image. Nathan and Mary J Berry Turley had four Children

item.title: Mary Kempe PAC
item.description:
Mary Kempe of Princess Anne County

Daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe, married Richard Williamson.

Mary Kempe from Princess Ann County Virginia#

PARENTS:

Mary’s Parents George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe

MARY KEMPE married RICHARD WILLIAMSON (daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe of Kempe’s Landing) There is some question at this time about Mary Kempe’s marriage date since George and Mary Hutchison Kempe may not have arrived in America before 1650. My thoughts are that Richard Williamson and Mary Kempe were probably married around 1670 to 1675. From Richard Williamson’s 1721 will we learn that his children were:

Born
c. 1872, Milton County, Georgia
Died
1958, buried at Harmony Grove Baptist Church Cemetery, Cobb County, Georgia
Married
1st: John Thomas Hartsfield (married 1887, died 1901) — 2nd: John England
Parents
Nathan Turley and Mary Jori Berry

item.title: Mary Polly Cates Georgia
item.description:
Parents: William Abraham Cates and Sarah Berry

Mary Polly Cates was born 10th January 1822 in South Carolina as her parents made their way to Georgia. She marries James Alexander Walton in 1848 shortly after his first wife died.

item.title: Nathaniel Arie Turley Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Nathaniel Arie Turley
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt Berry — Georgia
Gen 5Mary Jori Berry × Nathan Turley — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 6Nathaniel Arie Turley — Milton County, Georgia

Generation 6 Nathaniel Arie Turley

Born
c. 1873, Milton County, Georgia
Died
1937, buried in Ponder Cemetery
Married
1st: Mettie A. Haley (married November 25, 1900) — 2nd: Nancy B. Garrison (married January 6, 1926)
Parents
Nathan Turley and Mary Jori Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

NATHANIEL ARIE TURLEY was born about 1873 in Milton County Georgia. He died in 1937 and was buried in Ponder Cemetery. Arie, as he was known by the family, was married twice. He married Mettie A. Haley on November 25, 1900. When Mettie died, Arie married Nancy B. Garrison on January 6, 1926. There were no children born to either of these unions.

item.title: Proof Page
item.description:
Proof of the Berry-Williamson Connection

Evidence linking Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through the Williamson and Kempe families.

This page assembles the documentary evidence connecting Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through shared surnames, tax lists, and land records.

item.title: Ralph Radford Cates Texas and California
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Ralph Radford Cates
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Sarah Berry × William A. Cates — Clayton County, Georgia
Gen 5Mary Polly Cates × James Alexander Walton — Clayton County, Georgia
Gen 6James Thomas Cates × Stella Lavina George — Clayton County, GA → Cass County, Texas
Gen 7Ralph Radford Cates × Esta Era Collins — Cass County, Texas → California

Generation 7 Ralph Radford Cates

Born
November 14, 1898, Hughes Springs, Cass County, Texas
Died
Unknown
Married
Esta Era Collins (married October 7, 1920)
Parents
James Thomas Cates and Stella Lavina George
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Ralph Radford Cates#

Ralph Cates is the son of James Thomas Cates and Stella Lavina George. He was born in Hughes Springs, Cass County, Texas on November 14 1898. Cass County is the second County on the State line as you enter Texas in the north east corner of Texas and joining Oklahoma. Stella and her Family left Cass County at least 7 years before the great Dust Bowl disaster began in 1932. They were living in Mareno, Riverside California in 1930.

item.title: Robert Berry, Son of David J. and Mary Blalock
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (David J. line)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Eliza Everett — Orange County, NC → Fayette/Cobb County, Georgia

Generation 4 Robert Berry

Born
August 2, 1812, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
Georgia
Married
Eliza Everett (married June 16, 1833)
Parents
David J. Berry and Mary Blalock
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

ROBERT BERRY was born on August 2, 1812 in Orange County North Carolina was named for his grandfather Robert Berry who owned the Fiddleton Plantation. He was about sixteen years old when his father David J. Berry decided to remove the family to Georgia. Robert had an Uncle and some Berry Cousins in Fayette County Alabama, Wayne County Tennessee, and some Camp cousins in Georgia. David and his family apparently chose to settle near his sister, Mary Berry Camp’s children, in Fayette County Georgia.

item.title: Robert Riley Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Riley Berry Ga
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 5Robert Riley Berry × Martha Stark — Milton County, GA → Cullman County, AL

Generation 5 Robert Riley Berry

Born
June 10, 1840, Fayette County, Georgia
Died
March 31, 1909, Vinemont, Cullman County, Alabama
Married
Martha 'Mattie' Stark
Parents
Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Riley Berry#

Robert Riley Berry was born June 10, 1840 in Fayette County, Georgia. View Robert’s birthday entry in the family bible page. Robert Riley Berry grew up in the Roswell, Georgia area of Milton County, Georgia. In 1930 Milton County, Georgia was renamed Fulton County, Georgia. Robert Riley and two of his brothers married three sisters. The young ladies were the daughters of Jonas and Averella Kent Stark. Robert Riley married Martha “Mattie” Stark. The picture on the left is of Robert Riley and Mattie Stark Berry. Click on the picture to view a larger image. The other two brothers were William H. Berry who married Matilda Stark and John Allmon Berry who married Sarah “Sally” Stark. Robert died on March 31, 1909 in Vinemont, Cullman County, Alabama, USA[efn_note]Find A Grave profile for Robert Riley Berry[/efn_note].

item.title: Ruby Alyene Hunt (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Ruby Alyene Hunt
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Thomas Howard Barton × Mary Berry
Gen 5Joshua Crow × Mary Aliceann Elizabeth Barton
Gen 6Thomas C. Guthrie × Mary Frances Crow
Gen 7Lonnie Joseph Hunt × Lela Guthrie
Gen 8Ruby Alyene Hunt × Robert Raymond Vinson — Minden, Rusk County, TX

Generation 8 Ruby Alyene Hunt

Born
July 22, 1916, Minden, Rusk County, Texas
Died
November 7, 1982, Henderson, Rusk County, Texas
Married
Robert Raymond Vinson (b. November 25, 1903; d. June 13, 1979)
Parents
Lela Guthrie Hunt and Lonnie Joseph Hunt
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Ruby Alyene Hunt was born on July 22nd 1916 in Minden, Rusk County Texas and died on November 7th 1982 in Henderson, Rusk County Texas. She married Robert Raymond Vinson who was born on November 25th 1903 in New London, Rusk County Texas. Robert died on June 13th 1979 in Henderson, Rusk County Texas. They are buried in Pleasant Hill Cemetery between Henderson and Overton in Rusk County.

item.title: Sarah Berry Cates Ga
item.description:
Sarah Berry Cates (1796–1870)

Daughter of David J. and Mary Blalock Berry, married William Abraham Cates. Orange County, NC, to Clayton County, Georgia.

Sarah Berry (Cates), daughter of David J. and Mary Blalock Berry#

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry, Orange County, North Carolina, & Georgia.

Sarah Berry the daughter of David J. Berry and Mary Blalock married in Orange County, North Carolina on 7/26/1817 to William Abraham Cates. She was born April 4, 1796 in Orange County, North Carolina and died April 16, 1870 in Jonesboro, Clayton County, Georgia. William Abraham Cates was born Aug 2, 1798 in Orange County, North Carolina, and died March 28, 1871 in Jonesboro, Clayton County, Georgia.

September 16, 1907
Died

Married
Marvin Roger Snipes (married November 25, 1922)
Parents
Addie Lena Berry and Hampton Fantom Maners, Spalding, GA & Guilford County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Vera Othella Maners#

Marvin Roger Snipes Draft Registration for World War II#

Marvin Roger Snipes and Vera Othella Maners were married on November 25th 1922. Vera and Marvin made their home in High Point, North Carolina. The couple had one son, Marvin Roger Snipes Jr. who was born in 1927.

January 15, 1880, Etowah County, Alabama
Died

Married

Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Walter Glen Berry#

L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture

item.title: William Aaron Turley Ga
item.description:
William Aaron Turley (1879–1960)

Son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley, Georgia and Alabama.

William Aaron Turley, son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley#

Ancestors#

  • Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina
  • Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry: NC and Georgia
  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry: Georgia
  • Generation Mary Jori Berry and Nathan Turley: Alabama

WILLIAM AARON TURLEY born August 26, 1879 and died November 6, 1960. William Aaron Turley was the youngest child of Nathan and Mary J. Berry Turley. He was born on August 26th, 1876 according to his tombstone, however on the 1880 census he is listed as six months old. Since he left home at an early age he may have added three years to his age.

item.title: William Aaron Turley Ga
item.description:
William Aaron Turley

Son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley, Georgia and Alabama.

William Aaron Turley, son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley#

ANCESTORS

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry: NC and Georgia
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry Georgia
  5. Generation Mary Jori Berry and Nathan Turley Alabama

WILLIAM AARON TURLEY born August 26 1879 and died November 6, 1960. William Aaron Turley was the youngest child of Nathan and Mary J. Berry Turley. He was born on August 26th 1876 according to his tombstone, however on the 1880 census he is listed as six months old. Since he left home

at an early age he may have added three years to his age. It is not known exactly where he wandered before he finally settled in Alabama about 1908. The picture on the right is of Nathaniel Arie (on the left) and William Aaron Turley On the (right).Nathaniel Aire Berry is Will’s brother.This Picture was taken in the early 1920’s. .We know that William lived both in Blount and Morgan Counties Alabama before he married Emma Allena Berry. Emma who is the daughter of Robert Riley and Martha “Mattie” Stark Berry. Will, as he was known in Alabama, and Emma Turley had four children. Melvin Ray Berry died at birth.

item.description:
William Edgar Berry

item.title: William Lamar Berry
item.description:
William Lamar Berry

Son of Albert Edgar and Ella Clania Berry, Alabama.

William Lamar Berry, Son Of Albert Edgar And Ella Clania Berry#

ANCESTORS

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blaylock Berry: Orange County, North Carolina & GA.
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everett Berry: GA
  5. Generation Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry GA & Ala.
  6. Generation Albert Edgar and Ella Clania Berry

WILLIAM LAMAR BERRY was born on June 23, 1908 most likely in Muscogee County Georgia and died on March 30, 1990 in Tiverton, Newport, Rhode Island. William Lemar married Nannie Verna Mitchell Nannie Had two sisters Katie Mitchell and Virginia Mitchell. She had one brother Cecil (Thomas) Mitchell. Mitchell Ray Berry’s picture is on the right

item.title: William Oscar Berry GA
item.description:
William Oscar Berry

Son of Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry, Alabama.

ANCESTORS

  1. Generation Robert ans Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blaylock Berry, Orange County, North Carolina. & GA.
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everett Berry, NC and Georgia
  5. Jesse Lee and Nancy Henry Berry, Etowah Alabama and Nance, Muscogee, Georgia.
  6. William Oscar and Nettie Anderson Berry, Muscogee, Georgia.

L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture

item.title: William Thomas Maners
item.description:
William Thomas Maners

Berry family connection through Georgia.

Ancestors:

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County. Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. David J. and Mary Blaylock Berry, Orange County, North Carolina. & Georgia
  4. Robert and Elizabeth Everett Berry, Georgia, Fayette & Milton Counties.
  5. Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry, Georgia and Alabama
  6. Addie Lena Berry and Hampton Fantom Manners, Spaulding GA & Guilford County, North Carolina.

Front Row L-R Addie Lena Berry Maners, Vera Westbrooks Maners, wife of William Thomas Maners, Martin Maners. Second Row L-R Hampton F. Maners, Herman F. Maners, Jesse Waldo Maners.

item.title: Winifred Berry Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Winifred Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Winifred Berry × Joshua Norman — Orange County, NC → Henry County, Georgia

Generation 4 Winifred Berry

Born
North Carolina
Married
Joshua Norman (married September 19, 1820, Orange County, NC; died November 13, 1832, Henry County, GA)
Parents
David J. Berry and Mary Blalock
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Winifred Berry is the daughter of David J. Berry and Mary Blalock. She married Joshua Norman in Orange County, North Carolina on September 19 1820. Joshua was born in North Carolina in 1790 and died. Joshua Norman died on November 13 1832 in Henry County Georgia. Joshua Norman was a very young man when he died and the couple only had two children as far as we know at the time of this writing. Joshua Norman and his family probably went to Georgia sometime around 1828 when David J. Berry and the rest of his family did. David J. Berry and Joshua Norman Listed in 1830 Georgia census. Winifred Norman was living with her son, David Norman in the 1850 and 1860 Georgia Census’ Source Citation: Year: 1850; Census Place: District 29, Fayette, Georgia; Roll: M432_69; Page: 42; Image: 88. Source Citation: Year: 1860; Census Place: Northen District, Clayton, Georgia; Roll: M653_117; Page: 102; Image: 103.
